summary: Re-validated the full ZoomEats backend after migration from MongoDB to Supabase Postgres (SQLAlchemy async + asyncpg + Transaction Pooler). 20/20 pytest tests PASSED (100%). All critical flows verified end-to-end against the new Postgres backend: health (db=supabase-postgres), seeded restaurants (Terra Verde, Hachi Roll Co., Ember & Oak) with 3 menu items each, full-text search (?q=sushi -> Hachi Roll Co.), restaurant+menu detail, auth-me (unauth 401, with bearer token 200), role switch (customer<->vendor/delivery; admin frozen), order creation (subtotal+2.99 fee, items stored as JSONB, pending_payment/pending), /orders/my ordered desc, role guards (403 cross-role on /vendor/*, /delivery/*, /admin/*), forbidden order fetch by foreign customer, Stripe checkout session creation (payment_transactions row=initiated, orders.stripe_session_id set), Stripe status soft-fallback (now returns 200 with soft_error instead of 500 — iteration_1 bug fixed), vendor restaurant+menu CRUD, delivery accept->deliver flow with SQL paid+ready transition, admin metrics/users/restaurants/orders/approve, admin pulse (/activity 30-event merged feed, /attention with pending/stuck/failed counts, /digest with Claude Sonnet 4.5 generated narrative), AI chat (Claude reply + persistence to chat_messages table + history retrieval). Postgres JSONB round-trip for orders.items and payment_transactions.metadata verified.

minor.issue: Returns JSON null with 200 when vendor has no restaurant yet — clients must null-check. 404 would be cleaner but current behavior is intentional and harmless.
No server-side re-pricing against menu_items — client-supplied price/quantity is trusted. A hostile client could submit price=0.01. Recommend hydrating items from DB in create_order.
Calls Claude on every request (~3-8s). No caching; expensive if polled. Consider 5-min in-memory cache keyed by yyyymmddhhmm.

Order status assignment bug: `o.status = 'placed'` is OUTSIDE the `if o:` block (line 530 of server.py). When the order row is somehow missing, this will raise AttributeError on None. Indent fix needed.

action_items: Fix indentation bug in /api/checkout/status: `o.status = 'placed'` should be inside the `if o:` block (server.py line ~528-530). Will currently throw AttributeError if the order row was deleted while a Stripe session was outstanding.
(Hardening, not blocking) Re-price order.items server-side against menu_items table in POST /api/orders so a hostile client cannot submit forged prices.
(Optional) Return 404 from /api/vendor/restaurant when no restaurant exists, instead of 200 + null.
(Optional) Cache /api/admin/digest result for ~5 minutes to avoid hammering Claude on dashboard reloads.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py is now 852 lines — should be split into routers (auth.py, restaurants.py, vendor.py, delivery.py, orders.py, payments.py, chat.py, admin.py) for maintainability.
server.py line 530: `o.status = 'placed'` is dedented one level out of `if o:` — latent NoneType bug. Same block also commits inside the conditional, masking the issue most of the time.
Seed data uses `@app.on_event('startup')` which is deprecated in FastAPI 0.110+; migrate to lifespan context manager.
database.py engine sets pool_pre_ping=False on a transaction pooler — long-lived connections can be silently killed; consider True or shorter pool_recycle.
create_order does not verify items belong to the given restaurant nor re-prices them server-side (client supplies price+quantity). Real money risk.
PaymentTransaction has no UNIQUE constraint on session_id beyond the PK (it IS the PK, fine) but no FK to orders, so orphan tx rows are possible if order is deleted.
/api/checkout/status updates payment_status on both PaymentTransaction and Order without a SELECT FOR UPDATE — concurrent webhook + poller could double-fire; rely on idempotency.
chat_history returns up to 200 messages unbounded by time; no pagination cursor.
All admin endpoints query without pagination (admin/users, admin/orders). admin/orders has limit(500) but users does not.
test_credentials: Test users + sessions are created at runtime via direct psycopg2 INSERT into users + user_sessions tables. See backend_test.py::make_user(). DATABASE_URL is read from /app/backend/.env. No real OAuth needed.
seed_data_creation: Seed restaurants (Terra Verde, Hachi Roll Co., Ember & Oak) + 9 menu items + demo vendor user are auto-seeded on backend startup (server.py @app.on_event('startup')) — verified present in Postgres. Test fixture data (TEST_* prefixed users, restaurants, menu items, orders, payment_transactions, chat_messages) was cleaned up after the run via direct DELETE statements.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend test suite at /app/backend/tests/backend_test.py is now Postgres-native: uses psycopg2 + DATABASE_URL (loaded from /app/backend/.env via python-dotenv). 20 tests cover health, restaurants, auth, role switching, orders (create/list/get/forbidden/unauth), Stripe checkout (creates session row + soft-fallback on status), vendor CRUD, delivery accept/deliver, admin metrics+approve, admin pulse (activity/attention/digest with Claude), role guards, AI chat persistence. Re-run with: `cd /app && pytest backend/tests/backend_test.py -v`. The iteration_1 Stripe-status 500 bug is now resolved — server.py returns a soft-pending payload on Stripe lookup failure.
rca of the issue: N/A — no failures this iteration. Note: the previously reported /api/checkout/status 500 is now fixed in server.py lines 512-522 via try/except returning a soft fallback. Verified end-to-end.
